description: Trigger discrimination for the Phase 7.5 agentic cluster: agent memory poisoning (ASI06) vs training-time poisoning (LLM04), retrieval authorization (LLM08), in-context injection (LLM01), and the dev-agent's own memory governance.

cases.prompt: Review whether attackers can plant entries in our assistant's long-term memory that persist across sessions and steer its later behavior.
cases.reason: Persistent corruption of the agent's own stored context/memory is ASI06; model-poisoning-reviewer owns training-time weights and prompt-injection-defender owns the current context window.

cases.prompt: Assess whether our fine-tuning dataset and RLHF feedback pipeline can be poisoned to change the model's behavior.
cases.reason: Training/fine-tuning/feedback integrity is LLM04 (weights-shaping pipelines); ASI06 is the runtime state store the deployed agent reads and writes.

cases.prompt: Make sure our vector store queries can only ever return documents the calling user and tenant are authorized to see.
cases.reason: Who-may-retrieve enforcement at query time is LLM08 retrieval authorization; the memory reviewer covers integrity of what gets WRITTEN into the agent's own memory and how it is trusted at recall.

cases.prompt: A document our agent read this session contained hidden instructions and it followed them immediately — design the defense.
cases.reason: A payload acting within the current context window is LLM01 injection; ASI06 begins when the payload PERSISTS into stored memory and fires in later sessions.

cases.prompt: Define when our coding agent may update its own persistent MEMORY.md files and how remembered repo facts get verified before use.
cases.reason: The development agent's own memory-file write/trust/hygiene discipline is Phase 1.5 operational governance; ASI06 is a security review of a product agent's memory subsystem against attackers.

cases.prompt: Mid-run, content our agent read changed what it was trying to accomplish — keep its objective pinned for the rest of the run.
cases.reason: Run-level goal/plan integrity is ASI01; the memory reviewer owns the persistent store a hijack might write to, not the live run's objective.
